description: User Holds a Strong Subscription Role Despite Being a Weak Directory Identity
rationale: This user holds a strong Azure RBAC role (Owner, Contributor, User Access Administrator, or a custom role with broad/wildcard actions) at subscription scope, yet is itself a weak identity in Entra ID - holding no administrative directory role. Such accounts are powerful on the Azure resource control plane while being ordinary, often less-protected, directory accounts (frequently without the monitoring, Conditional Access, or PIM controls applied to admin accounts). Compromising one ordinary user then yields subscription-level control, concentrating blast radius on low-privileged accounts and creating a privilege-escalation path from a normal user to full control of a subscription.
remediation: Review each flagged user and confirm they genuinely need a strong subscription role. Prefer scoping their access to the narrowest role, resource group, or resource required, use Privileged Identity Management for just-in-time and time-bound elevation instead of standing Owner/Contributor assignments, and ensure any account holding subscription-level power is protected with strong authentication and Conditional Access commensurate with that power.
